metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: status_code: 429, model_name: claude-sonnet-4-6, body: {'detail': 'Rate limited. Retry after 0.1s'}

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific trigger patterns / phrase templates that constitute the 'confident-assertion lexicon' in the plan (e.g. 'X is already implemented', 'Y handles Z', 'no need to …') so the executor has no design choices left to make.
findings.finding: The 'confident-assertion trigger lexicon' for E4 is described in purpose but its actual entries/patterns are not enumerated — the executor must choose what constitutes an 'assertion-shaped sentence', which is a real design decision deferred rather than made.
findings.location: What > G-7(a); Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Lexicon entries (starter set)' subsection under G-7(a) listing concrete regex or phrase templates, e.g.: `already (exists|implemented|done)`, `handled by \w+`, `no .* needed`, `will (just|simply) work`, `assumes \w+`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the dedup rule for exec:DET vs E6.no_hedges (e.g. 'suppress DET finding if E6 already emits a finding for the same sentence span', or 'DET findings are always emitted; Pass 2 merger drops duplicates by location+keyword key').
findings.finding: The dedup mechanism between exec:DET and E6.no_hedges is unspecified — 'DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges' names the goal but leaves the executor to choose the dedup strategy (same-location suppression? same-hedge-word suppression? post-hoc merge?).
findings.location: What > G-7(b); Acceptance Criteria AC3

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or fixture per AC — e.g. 'run E4 scan on fixture_plan_with_assertions.md and assert ≥1 finding with tag=assertion_probe' for AC1, and 'run DET on fixture_with_hedge.md and assert finding routes to WS1 sub-answer key' for AC3.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has a concrete proving command or test case — none of the four ACs specifies how an executor would produce evidence that the criterion is met (what to run, what input to use, what output to observe).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC4 as a concrete, deterministic criterion: specify a fixture containing known nit-hedges (e.g. 'this is a well-formed assumption with documented fallback') and assert that the output contains zero findings (or findings with validity=low that are dropped by Pass 2), runnable via a named command.
findings.finding: AC4 ('Nits verified as dropped') is effort/process-framed ('verified as dropped') without specifying what input stimulus, what observable output, and what threshold constitute passing — making it non-evaluable in-session by a deterministic command.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria > AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Example rewrite: 'Running exec:DET on fixture_nit_hedges.md (containing only rhetorical hedges and well-formed assumptions) produces either zero findings or all findings with validity < threshold, verified by `pytest tests/test_det_nit_drop.py`.`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion explicitly verifying that the E4 scope-exclusion sub-check emits an abstain-with-coverage result (not a hard finding) when the exclusion claim references an external fact not checkable in the codebase.
findings.finding: G-4's 'abstain-with-coverage for external-fact exclusions' behavior is described in the What section but has no corresponding acceptance criterion verifying it; this is an uncovered deliverable.
findings.location: What section, G-4 paragraph / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] E4 scope-exclusion sub-check: external-fact exclusions produce abstain-with-coverage, not a finding'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'well-formed assumptions with fallback' in the What section (or inline in the AC) — e.g. 'an assumption explicitly paired with a stated mitigation/contingency' — so the dropped-nit criterion is verifiable.
findings.finding: AC #4 introduces the concept 'well-formed assumptions with fallback' as a verified-dropped case, but this concept is not described or defined anywhere in the What section, leaving its meaning and test conditions ambiguous.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, item 4: 'Nits (rhetorical hedges, well-formed assumptions with fallback) verified as dropped'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/G-7(b) description, add: 'well-formed assumptions (those paired with an explicit fallback or contingency) are treated as non-substantive and should receive low/no validity score from the verifier.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a small fixture or RED-test (a handful of labeled plan sentences) to validate confident-assertion and hedge-word lexicon recall/precision before full rollout.
findings.finding: The confident-assertion trigger lexicon and hedge-word list are asserted heuristics with no measurement or validation plan — their recall/precision against real plan text is unknown and untested.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(a) and G-7(b) description
findings.suggested_fix: Create a short labeled fixture (e.g., 10–15 plan sentences, tagged as should-fire / should-not-fire) and run both lexicons against it as part of the acceptance test for G-7(a) and G-7(b). Record hit-rate and false-positive rate; adjust lexicon iteratively until metrics meet a stated threshold.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Externalize both lexicons into a configurable location (config file or named constant block) and add an ADR/CLAUDE.md note explaining exec:DET's role relative to E6.no_hedges and the dedup contract.
findings.finding: The confident-assertion and hedge-word lexicons are likely to evolve over time (new assertion forms, domain-specific hedges) but the plan does not specify whether they will be configurable or hardcoded constants.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(a) and G-7(b); Acceptance Criteria

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify error handling for E4's Grep/Read probes: define the graceful-degradation path (e.g., log + abstain on I/O error, surface a coverage-gap flag) so failures are visible and the sub-answer remains trustworthy.
findings.finding: E4's scan protocol relies on Grep/Read calls to probe each enumerated assertion; no error handling is specified for I/O failures (file not found, permission error, empty result), leaving the sub-check's failure mode unaddressed.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(a): 'probe each via Grep/Read'
findings.suggested_fix: On any Grep/Read exception or empty/error result, log a structured warning (file path, assertion text, error type), mark that assertion as 'probe-failed', and include a probe_failures_count field in the sub-answer so WS1 can distinguish 'checked and clean' from 'could not check'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the concurrency/ownership model for writing to the shared sub-answer: specify whether writes are append-only with dedup-by-key, who serializes concurrent E4 and exec:DET writers, and whether the operation is idempotent under retry.
findings.finding: The plan introduces two new writers (E4 and exec:DET) to the shared sub-answer 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim' without specifying concurrency ownership or atomicity: who holds the write lock, whether writes are append-only vs. merge, and whether concurrent E4+DET writes can interleave or corrupt the sub-answer state.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(b) and overall 'feeding the shared sub-answer'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify that all findings are appended as keyed records (finding_id = source + location hash) and that the sub-answer aggregator deduplicates by key at read time, making individual writes safe to retry and compose without coordination locks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism — e.g., a before/after comparison on a representative sample (N plans) showing that committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim findings increase or false-positive rate stays bounded, or an operational metric target for the hedge-detector mis-flag drop rate.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is named in any acceptance criterion: there is no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding step that would let a reviewer confirm the improvement delivered observable value to the pipeline's consumers.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'On a held-out set of ≥10 plans with known unbacked claims, the combined E4+DET pipeline surfaces ≥X% more committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim findings than the baseline, with rhetorical-hedge false-positive rate ≤Y%.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the exec:DET hedge detector can reuse the existing `det_floor.py` P6 path (extending `_VAGUE_LEXICON` with hedge words) instead of adding a new DET criterion that would require plan-text scanning in Engine B — the current `exec:DET` / `det_invariants` pipeline scans code files over `repo_root`, not plan-text strings (`det_invariants.py:engine_b.scan(repo_root, ...)`), so routing a plan-text hedge check through `exec:DET` would either require a temp-file workaround or new non-codebase plumbing not present in the codebase.
findings.finding: The proposed `exec:DET` hedge-word detector targets plan text (ticket description strings), but the existing `exec:DET` infrastructure (Engine B / `det_invariants.run_project_det_checks`) scans *code files* via opengrep/ast-grep/metric over a `repo_root` filesystem — it has no 'scan plan text' backend. Using it for hedge-word detection in plan text would require either a temp-file workaround or new non-codebase text-scan plumbing, neither of which is present. Meanwhile, P6 (`det_floor.py:_VAGUE_LEXICON`) already performs lexical scanning of plan text for vague/hedge-like terms, and E6's `no_hedges` sub-check (`criteria_routing.json:224`) already covers hedge detection at the LLM tier. Adding a new `exec:DET` path for plan-text hedge words rebuilds plan-text scanning already handled by P6, without justifying why P6 extension is insufficient.
findings.location: What section — 'G-7(b): exec:DET hedge-word detector'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) implement as a P6 extension by adding hedge words ('probably', 'assume', 'should be', 'seems') to `det_floor.py:_VAGUE_LEXICON` — no new infrastructure, routes naturally through the existing P6 advisory advisory path — or (b) explicitly specify how the DET machinery is extended to scan plan-text strings rather than code files, and justify why P6 extension is insufficient.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` is an existing binary sub-answer in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`'s `GRADED_BINARY`, or if it must be added — if the latter, add an explicit AC covering the kernel vocabulary and `verification_model()` schema change.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that E4 and exec:DET findings will feed 'WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim sub-answer', treating this as an existing named construct in the shared kernel. No such sub-answer exists anywhere in the codebase: the binary sub-answer vocabulary in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py is the closed set {is_verifiable, evidence_entails_finding, path_reachable, impact_follows_necessarily, no_viable_alternative_explanation, no_existing_mitigation, severity_claim_justified}, and zero hits for 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim' appear in src/, docs/, or tests/. The plan appears to be naming a concept it intends to CREATE while presenting it as already committed — an unverifiable codebase assertion.
findings.location: What section, bullet 3: 'exec:DET hedge-word detector … emitting findings that flow through Pass 2. DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges … The verifier judges SUBSTANCE (committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim)'
findings.suggested_fix: If this is a new sub-answer to be introduced, add an AC: '[ ] New binary sub-answer `committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim` added to GRADED_BINARY in decide.py and verification_model() in verify.py, with tests'. If the plan instead means exec:DET findings route to an existing sub-answer (e.g. evidence_entails_finding or no_existing_mitigation), name that sub-answer explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the characterisation of E6.no_hedges: confirm from `criteria_routing.json` that its scope is limited to ACs using hedges instead of proving commands, then re-specify the exec:DET dedup boundary to reflect the actual (narrower) overlap.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges (which already does LLM-tier hedge detection)' — but E6.no_hedges is scoped specifically to ACs that use hedges instead of proving commands (criteria_routing.json: 'No red-flag hedges standing in for a proving command'). It does not scan plan-body assumption hedges. The dedup rationale therefore rests on a partial/incorrect characterisation of what E6.no_hedges checks, and the actual overlap between the two detectors is narrower than claimed.
findings.location: What section, bullet 3 (G-7(b)): 'DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges (which already does LLM-tier hedge detection)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges (which already does LLM-tier hedge detection)' with a precise scope statement, e.g.: 'exec:DET targets plan-body hedges ('probably', 'assume', 'should be', 'seems'); E6.no_hedges targets AC hedges standing in for proving commands — overlap is limited to AC lines containing both patterns; dedup only those.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim' names an existing binary sub-answer key (it does not appear in src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py GRADED_BINARY); either correct the name to an existing GRADED_BINARY key (e.g. 'is_verifiable') or explicitly scope this as a CREATION of a new sub-answer field and enumerate the files to modify (decide.py, verify.py, verifier prompt, schema).
findings.finding: 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim' is named as a pre-existing 'shared sub-answer' that DET findings will be 'routed to', but this symbol does not exist anywhere in src/. The real binary sub-answer vocabulary is GRADED_BINARY in review_kernel/decide.py ('is_verifiable', 'evidence_entails_finding', etc.) — no such field exists.
findings.location: What section — G-7(b): 'routed to WS1 substance sub-answer'; AC line 'exec:DET hedge detector, deduped vs E6.no_hedges, routed to WS1 substance sub-answer'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'routed to WS1 substance sub-answer' with the concrete existing field name (e.g. 'is_verifiable' or 'evidence_entails_finding' from GRADED_BINARY), or add an explicit AC item for creating the new sub-answer field and list the affected files.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly classify each What hunk as CREATION (new behavior added to existing criterion file or as new DET criterion) and enumerate the specific files each will modify: plan_review_E4.md, criteria_routing.json (E4 checklist), det_floor.py or det_invariants.py for the hedge detector.
findings.finding: All three behavioral hunks in the What section (G-7(a) confident-assertion lexicon, G-4 scope-exclusion sub-check, G-7(b) exec:DET hedge detector) are new behavior not present in the codebase, making them CREATION/out-of-scope per the rubric — but the plan does not classify or acknowledge them as new additions.
findings.location: What section — all three bullet items

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the consumers of E4's criteria_routing.json checklist and plan_review_E4.md that need updating: at minimum the plan-review workflow gate (plan-review.yaml), the plan_review registry tests (test_plan_review.py), and the production batch runner tests (test_production_batch_runner.py).
findings.finding: The plan names files/constructs it will modify (E4 prompt, criteria_routing.json, the exec:DET mechanism) but does not enumerate consumers outside src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ that depend on the E4 checklist schema or routing JSON — specifically: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml (routes E4), tests/unit/test_plan_review.py (indexes E4 by id), tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py (asserts E4 tier), and the agentic harness files.
findings.location: What section — G-7(a) and G-4 E4 modifications

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the data-flow for the DET hedge detector: DET findings bypass Pass 2 (verify) entirely in the current architecture (workflow_ops.py:91-94) — either (a) specify how nit-suppression works without validity scoring, or (b) reconsider whether hedge detection should be an AGENT-tier E4 checklist item (where the verifier CAN apply the validity gate and judge rhetorical vs. substantive).
findings.finding: The plan states the exec:DET hedge detector's findings 'flow through Pass 2' — but in the actual codebase, DET findings are routed AROUND Pass 2 (verify). det_floor results go directly into partition_findings via det_blocking_findings/det_advisory_findings (workflow_ops.py:91-94); Pass 2 (verify_findings) only processes LLM-tier findings. The 'mis-flags get low validity → dropped' mechanism the plan relies on for nit suppression does not apply to DET-tier findings — they carry no validity score and are not subject to binary sub-answer scoring.
findings.location: What section, G-7(b): 'exec:DET hedge-word detector … emitting findings that flow through Pass 2'
findings.suggested_fix: Add hedge detection as a new checklist item in E4's AGENT-tier rubric (plan_review_E4.md) rather than as a separate exec:DET criterion. E4 already scans for 'hedges/confident-assertions', runs through Pass 2 with full validity scoring, and the AGENT tier can distinguish rhetorical hedges from substantive unbacked claims — eliminating both the DET data-flow mismatch and the dedup problem.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale for the exec:DET hedge-detector approach (why a deterministic pattern-match is preferable to extending E4's existing hedge-scanning, given that E4 is already AGENT-tier and already mentions 'hedges/confident-assertions' in its rubric).
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for WHY the exec:DET tier is the right mechanism for hedge detection. The only approach-selection language ('cheaper/earlier DET signal, not a duplicate') is a contrast with E6, not an affirmative case for a deterministic pattern-match over an LLM-judgment approach — especially given that hedges require context to judge (rhetorical vs. substantive), which a DET pattern-matcher cannot provide.
findings.location: What section, G-7(b); plan lacks a positive rationale section for the DET approach choice

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the exec:DET hedge-word detector's execution host can scan plan-prose (not codebase files): either confirm engine_b / det_invariants supports a plan-text scan target, or specify the alternative execution path (e.g. a new DET check in det_floor.py similar to P6/P7, rather than a project DET criterion).
findings.finding: The exec:DET hedge-word detector is proposed to scan plan-prose for hedge words, but the existing det_invariants._run_one infrastructure calls engine_b.scan(repo_root, registry=reg_slice) — which scans codebase *files*, not a plan-text string. Routing a plan-text regex scan through the project DET/engine_b pipeline is an unverified capability: the detector backend (opengrep/ast-grep) targets source files, not the in-memory plan text passed at review time.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(b): exec:DET hedge-word detector
findings.suggested_fix: Model the hedge detector as a new static DET floor check (alongside P6/P7 in det_floor.py) that scans ctx.plan_text with a regex — this is exactly the pattern P6 uses for _VAGUE_LEXICON and P7 uses for _DESTRUCTIVE_RE. This avoids the engine_b/file-scan mismatch and reuses the established plan-text scanning infrastructure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the claim that exec:DET hedge findings 'flow through Pass 2' with the actual DET finding lifecycle (DET findings bypass the LLM verifier); either clarify that the hedge detector is LLM-tier (e.g. a new sub-check on E6) or drop the Pass-2 routing claim and rely on the advisory posture + advisory-cap to suppress low-signal mis-flags.
findings.finding: The plan claims DET hedge-detector findings 'flow through Pass 2' ('emitting findings that flow through Pass 2'), but the existing DET tier (det_floor + det_invariants) produces findings that bypass the LLM verifier entirely — they are merged directly into the verdict partition as DET-tier results by the orchestrator, not routed through pass2_verify. The claim about Pass-2 routing is inconsistent with the existing DET finding lifecycle.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(b) and Acceptance Criteria: 'exec:DET hedge detector, deduped vs E6.no_hedges, routed to WS1 substance sub-answer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assess the gap between the existing E4 prompt ('Scan... hedges/confident-assertions... FORCE a Grep/Read probe') and the proposed lexicon-based SCAN PROTOCOL before committing — state specifically what the current prompt lacks that the enumerable lexicon addresses.
findings.finding: The plan's G-7(a) 'confident-assertion trigger lexicon as SCAN PROTOCOL' for E4 may be re-specifying what the existing E4 prompt already covers: the current plan_review_E4.md prompt already reads 'Scan the plan for assertions about the codebase ("X already exists", "Y does Z", hedges/confident-assertions) and FORCE a Grep/Read probe per assertion', and the criteria_routing.json checklist already has 'assertions_probed'. The plan does not assess whether the existing E4 prompt is deficient and needs a lexicon, or whether this is incremental refinement — relevant prior art within the codebase is not compared.
findings.location: ## What — G-7(a): E4 confident-assertion scan-protocol lexicon

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that exec:DET is an existing, reusable executor interface in the codebase (cite a prior detector that uses it, or a --help / framework doc) before committing the hedge-detector AC; if no prior usage exists, route to a SPIKE first.
findings.finding: The plan introduces 'exec:DET' as a new detector type without verifying that the DET executor/framework actually exists and supports the described registration pattern. The plan asserts this as a buildable component ('exec:DET hedge detector') but provides no evidence the DET execution tier is an established, reusable interface in the codebase rather than a capability gap requiring a spike.
findings.location: What section / G-7(b): 'exec:DET hedge-word detector'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the complete confident-assertion trigger lexicon (the exact phrase list, e.g. 'already in repo', 'loaded from', 'reuses', 'provided by', 'is fetched from', 'already does') directly in the updated E4 rubric or checklist, co-located with the scan-protocol description.
findings.finding: The 'confident-assertion trigger lexicon' is named as E4's new SCAN PROTOCOL but its value vocabulary is never enumerated in the plan. The existing plan_review_E4.md prompt is a single-sentence rubric with no explicit phrase list; the experimental harness (exp2_agentic.py line 14) has an inline ad-hoc list but there is no canonical lexicon defined anywhere. An executor implementing G-7(a) has no defined set of trigger phrases to compile into the protocol.
findings.location: What / G-7(a); AC: 'E4 confident-assertion scan-protocol lexicon'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit `trigger_lexicon` sub-list to `plan_review_E4.md`'s checklist section, sourced from the validated exp2_agentic.py phrase set: 'already in repo', 'loaded from', 'reuses', 'provided by', 'is fetched from', 'already does' — and enumerate any new additions for the scope-exclusion sub-check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either identify the existing verifier sub-answer key that corresponds to 'committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim' (and name it precisely in the plan), or specify it as a new sub-answer addition including its closed vocabulary {yes|no|insufficient}, fail-safe default, and coercion rule.
findings.finding: The plan references 'WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim sub-answer' as an existing named schema construct that DET hedge detector findings 'flow through'. This identifier does not exist anywhere in the codebase: searching src/, docs/, and tests/ yields zero matches. The verifier sub-answer schema (plan_review_verifier.md, verify.py) uses named fields like evidence_entails_finding, significance_of_finding, etc. — none matching this key. The plan relies on a non-existent schema reference.
findings.location: What / G-7(b): 'emitting findings that flow through Pass 2'; AC: 'exec:DET hedge detector, deduped vs E6.no_hedges, routed to WS1 substance sub-answer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete dedup mechanism between the exec:DET hedge detector and E6.no_hedges: e.g., does E6.no_hedges add an ANTI-FP clause 'skip if a DET hedge finding already covers this word', or does Pass-2 novelty detection suppress the duplicate, or is there a routing exclusion?
findings.finding: The dedup mechanism between the exec:DET hedge detector and E6.no_hedges is stated as a goal ('DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges') but no protocol is specified. exec:DET runs in the deterministic phase (orchestrator.py:348-353) before LLM criteria; E6 runs as a 1-TURN LLM criterion in a separate batch. There is no existing cross-tier dedup path in the framework. The plan does not specify how dedup is implemented: whether it's a routing exclusion, a Pass-2 novelty check, a filter on E6's sub-answer scope, or something else.
findings.location: What / G-7(b): 'DEDUP against existing E6.no_hedges'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the discrimination rule for the G-4 scope-exclusion sub-check: specify (a) what makes an exclusion claim 'empirically-checkable' (e.g., references a file/symbol/module → grep-probeable), (b) what makes it an 'external-fact' exclusion (e.g., references a human decision, external system, or business rule), and (c) the checklist key name to add to E4's schema.
findings.finding: The G-4 scope-exclusion sub-check introduces a decision rule — 'abstain-with-coverage for external-fact exclusions' vs. fire on 'empirically-checkable (codebase) exclusions' — but provides no discrimination criterion co-located with the sub-check schema. The existing E4 checklist in criteria_v8.json has three keys (assertions_probed, fail_closed, read_before_flag); the plan adds a new sub-check but does not define its checklist key, its rule for distinguishing codebase vs. external-fact exclusion claims, or its abstain output shape.
findings.location: What / G-4: 'E4 sub-check for scope-exclusion / descoping claims'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
